bibliographic_data.title: Emo2Vec: Learning Generalized Emotion Representation by Multi-task Training
bibliographic_data.abstract: In this paper, we propose Emo2Vec which encodes emotional semantics into vectors. We train Emo2Vec by multi-task learning six different emotion-related tasks, including emotion/sentiment analysis, sarcasm classification, stress detection, abusive language classification, insult detection, and personality recognition. Our evaluation of Emo2Vec shows that it outperforms existing affect-related representations, such as Sentiment-Specific Word Embedding and DeepMoji embeddings with much smaller training corpora. When concatenated with GloVe, Emo2Vec achieves competitive performances to state-of-the-art results on several tasks using a simple logistic regression classifier.

core_content.novelty_claim: Emo2Vec encodes emotional semantics into vectors through multi-task learning of six different emotion-related tasks, outperforming existing affect-related representations with much smaller training corpora.
core_content.key_methodology: Emo2Vec is trained using an end-to-end multi-task learning framework with a shared embedding layer and task-specific classifiers. The model uses a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for the larger dataset and logistic regression for smaller datasets.
outcomes: Emo2Vec outperforms existing affect-related representations on multiple tasks
Emo2Vec achieves competitive performance to state-of-the-art results when combined with GloVe
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, the authors propose Emo2Vec which encodes emotional semantics into vectors. They train Emo2Vec by multi-task learning six different emotion-related tasks including emotion/sentiment analysis, sarcasm classification, stress detection, abusive language classification, insult detection, and personality recognition. The evaluation of Emo2Vec shows that it outperforms existing affect-related representations such as Sentiment-Specific Word Embedding and DeepMoji embeddings with much smaller training corpora. When concatenated with GloVe, Emo2Vec achieves competitive performances to state-of-the-art results on several tasks using a simple logistic regression classifier.
